mnemonics: P(A|B) = P(B|A) * P(A) / P(B) - Remember: Posterior = (Likelihood * Prior) / Evidence
keyPoints: Bayes' Rule updates the probability of a hypothesis based on new evidence.
It combines prior knowledge (P(A)) with new data (P(B|A)) to form a posterior probability (P(A|B)).
It is fundamental in many AI applications, including spam filtering, medical diagnosis, and machine learning algorithms.
commonMistakes: Confusing P(A|B) with P(B|A) (the prosecutor's fallacy).
Forgetting to account for the base rate (prior probability) of an event.
Incorrectly assuming events are independent when they are not.
